rationale: The cited paper is explicitly invoked in the methods/results section as a source for the spotlight analysis procedure used to probe interaction effects in Experiment 2. This is more than a background mention because it guides how the authors test and interpret their moderated regression results. Although it appears in a grouped citation with Aiken and West (1991), the function is still clear and methodological.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Irwin and McClelland (2001) as methodological guidance for analyzing interaction terms using spotlight/simple-slope style probing in its moderated regression tests. The citation supports statistical interpretation rather than the paper's core psychological theory.
